shots.title: Template generators replace static templates
shots.notes: Created template-generator.ts with 10 generator functions for all platform/format combos. Refactored init.ts to use generators instead of cpSync from static templates. 15 new tests.

shots.title: Cursor .cursorrules + complete integration
shots.notes: Added generateCursorrules() for project-root .cursorrules file with metaphor-aware vocabulary. slope init --cursor now installs .cursorrules + .mdc rules + MCP config. 6 new tests.

shots.title: OpenCode AGENTS.md + opencode.json MCP config
shots.notes: Added --opencode provider. Generates AGENTS.md (OpenCode's project context) and opencode.json with MCP config using OpenCode's format (type: local, command array). 7 new tests.

shots.title: Platform detection + unified init
shots.notes: Auto-detection from project markers (.claude/, .cursor/, opencode.json, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .cursorrules). --all flag installs for all 3 platforms. 10 new tests.

conditions.description: Context continued from previous session — required reconstruction from summary

nutrition.description: Full build+test after every ticket (554 tests at completion)
Commit-per-ticket discipline — 4 commits for 4 tickets, pushed after each
38 new tests across CLI (15 generator + 6 cursor + 7 opencode + 10 detection)
S9-1 refactored init.ts cleanly — existing 11 init tests all pass unchanged

nineteenth_hole.how_did_it_feel: Cleanest sprint yet — zero hazards across all 4 tickets. The template generator pattern from S9-1 made S9-2 and S9-3 trivial to implement since the generator functions were already structured for new platforms.
nineteenth_hole.advice_for_next_player: OpenCode uses a different MCP config format than Claude Code and Cursor: command is an array (not string+args), top-level key is 'mcp' (not 'mcpServers'), and type field is required. Always research platform config formats before implementing.
nineteenth_hole.what_surprised_you: No hazards at all. The S8 metaphor engine was so well-designed that plugging it into template generation was completely smooth. The MetaphorDefinition interface provided exactly the vocabulary needed.
nineteenth_hole.excited_about_next: Three platforms now fully supported. The generator pattern means adding a fourth platform in the future is just adding one more generator function + install handler.

yardage_book_updates: template-generator.ts: 10 generator functions covering CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .cursorrules, sprint-checklist, commit-discipline, review-loop (3 formats each)
OpenCode MCP format: {mcp: {slope: {type: 'local', command: ['npx', '@srbryers/mcp-tools']}}} in opencode.json
init --all installs for all 3 platforms; no-flag auto-detects from project markers
course_management_notes: 4 tickets, 4 in_the_hole — zero misses, zero hazards
Tests grew from 516 to 554 (+38 across CLI)
